title: XR-85 tank droid

{{Quote|Take out those tanks!|[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], upon seeing the tanks firing on the ''Nonnah''|<ref name="RS I">[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]</ref>}}
Manufactured by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]], the '''XR-85 tank droid''' was a treaded, automated tank that was part of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] arsenal.

==Description==
The [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] XR-85 [[Tank/Legends|tank]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was armed with a [[heavy particle cannon]], two forward light [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], four twin heavy [[Repeating blaster/Legends|repeating blasters]] (two at the front, two at the back), and one aft [[Golan Arms/Legends|Golan Arms]] [[DF.9 laser cannon]] (for anti-personnel duty).<ref name="DES" />

==History==
[[File:TankDroid_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|180px|XR-85 tank droid [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]
The earliest models were roughly the same size as the [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|All Terrain Personal Transport]], allowing multiple units to be carried inside a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]]. They were used to protect bases, attack [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] uprisings, and keep the peace on Imperial [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. However, the prototypes were too expensive for mass production.

Around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], three XR-85s were deployed to [[Search for the Nonnah|attack]] the wrecked Rebel [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Nonnah]]''. Around the same time, a group protected the [[Weapons Research Facility]] on [[Fest/Legends|Fest]]. Many of those units were destroyed in [[Escape from Fest|battle]] there with [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].<ref name="RS I" />

Later models, produced after [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] return and subsequent campaign, were over twice the size of a standard Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] and more inexpensive and practical to mass-produce. They utilized a pirated [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]] [[R7 droid brain matrix]], used in the [[R7-series astromech droid]], which made each droid far more independent and intuitive than earlier generations of combat droids.<ref name="TEGtVaV" />

Tank droids fought on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the [[Imperial Civil War]].<ref name="DE1">[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]]</ref>

Cost-cutting by the crumbling Empire made the XR-85s a good alternative to other assault vehicles, as it became more difficult to find or train qualified crew. The production of tank droids was increased in order to bolster ground forces wherever possible.

[[File:XR-85_Coruscant.jpg|thumb|right|200px|What became known as a partially damaged XR-85 fighting on Coruscant was actually intended to be an Imperial vehicle positioned between a cannon tower and a destroyed [[TIE crawler/Legends|TIE crawler]]]]

==Behind the scenes==
The XR-85 tank droid first appeared in the [[1991]] comic book [[Dark Empire 1|''Dark Empire'' 1]],<ref name="DE1" /> and was later named in the [[1993]] ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''.<ref name="DES" /> The illustrations of the tank droid in ''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''<ref name="TEGtVaV" /> was based on a panel from ''Dark Empire'' 1 that showed a tracked Imperial vehicle in front of a tower emplacement, which was misinterpreted as a single vehicle;<ref name="DE1" /> the tracked vehicle appeared separately in [[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]].<ref name="DE4">[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]]</ref> However, all subsequent depictions of the XR-85 tank droid have been based on the combined vehicle design, such as its appearance in the [[1998]] video game [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']].<ref name="RS I" />
